task: Supabase Google OAuth + session management in Python FastAPI

recommended_mcps.name: supabase
capabilities: Enable Supabase Auth providers (Google OAuth) via dashboard API
Read/write kg_users table and auth.users directly
Inspect RLS policies and auth configuration
Fetch project ref and service keys needed for server-side auth
recommended_mcps.why_needed: Supabase Auth configuration (enabling Google OAuth provider, setting redirect URLs, managing auth.users table) can be done via the Supabase MCP without leaving the editor. Also needed to run the user table migration SQL.
recommended_mcps.install_method: Already in .mcp.json — needs SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN env var to activate

recommended_mcps.name: playwright
capabilities: Screenshot the login flow (Google OAuth redirect)
Verify the /auth/callback route handles the code exchange
Test session cookie persistence across page reloads
Verify protected routes redirect unauthenticated users
recommended_mcps.why_needed: OAuth flows require a real browser to exercise the redirect chain. Playwright is the only tool available that can drive the Google OAuth consent screen and verify the callback handler works end-to-end.
recommended_mcps.install_method: Already in .mcp.json — no extra env vars needed

recommended_mcps.name: render
capabilities: Set SUPABASE_URL / SUPABASE_ANON_KEY / SESSION_SECRET env vars on Render service
Trigger redeploy after env var changes
Read runtime logs to debug OAuth callback errors in production
recommended_mcps.why_needed: OAuth redirect URIs must match the production URL on Render. New env vars (GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET, SESSION_SECRET) need to be pushed to Render without manual dashboard work.
recommended_mcps.install_method: Already active — use mcp__render__update_environment_variables and mcp__render__list_logs

recommended_mcps.name: github
capabilities: Commit auth implementation files to the repo
Create PR for the auth feature branch
Search existing code for session/auth patterns
recommended_mcps.why_needed: Standard version control for the implementation. Supabase cloud mode relies on GitHub for note sync, so any schema changes must be committed.

recommended_mcps.name: context7
capabilities: Fetch latest supabase-py auth API docs
recommended_mcps.why_needed: supabase-py auth API and gotrue-py session handling have changed significantly since training cutoff. Context7 resolves the correct current method signatures.
recommended_mcps.install_method: Already active — quota resets monthly; use sparingly for targeted lookups
recommended_mcps.note: Monthly quota exhausted as of this run. Resolved /tomasvotava/fastapi-sso and /fastapi/fastapi IDs for future queries.

recommended_skills.name: superpowers:brainstorming
recommended_skills.why_needed: Design the auth flow (OAuth redirect, callback, session storage strategy, RLS policy) before writing code

recommended_skills.name: superpowers:writing-plans
recommended_skills.why_needed: Produce a structured, testable implementation plan: SQL migration, FastAPI routes, session middleware, frontend JS, tests

recommended_skills.name: superpowers:test-driven-development
recommended_skills.why_needed: Auth code is security-critical — write failing tests for each route (callback, /me, protected endpoints) before implementing

recommended_skills.name: superpowers:subagent-driven-development
recommended_skills.why_needed: Auth implementation has 4+ independent work streams (SQL schema, FastAPI routes, session middleware, frontend JS) that can be parallelised

recommended_skills.name: superpowers:requesting-code-review
recommended_skills.why_needed: Security review of the OAuth implementation — check for CSRF on callback, cookie flags (httponly/secure/samesite), RLS policy gaps

recommended_skills.name: superpowers:verification-before-completion
recommended_skills.why_needed: Run full test suite and verify no regressions before declaring auth complete

recommended_agents.name: code-reviewer
recommended_agents.why_needed: Security-focused review of auth routes — CSRF token, cookie flags, Supabase RLS policies, redirect URL validation

gaps_and_blockers.action_required: Set SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN in shell env or .env before pipeline execution. Token is a Supabase personal access token from https://supabase.com/dashboard/account/tokens
Create OAuth 2.0 credentials in Google Cloud Console. Add Render production URL as authorised redirect URI: https://<app>.onrender.com/auth/callback
Either wait for quota reset or add a Context7 API key at https://context7.com/dashboard. Fallback: use supabase-py GitHub source directly for auth API examples.
Not needed — frontend is vanilla HTML/CSS/JS, no React component library required

notes: The Supabase MCP (@supabase/mcp-server-supabase) is already declared in .mcp.json but is NOT currently active in this session — it needs SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN to connect. This is the single most valuable tool for this task: it can enable Google OAuth on the Supabase project, run the users table SQL migration, and inspect RLS policies without leaving the editor. Playwright (also in .mcp.json) is essential for verifying the OAuth redirect chain end-to-end. All other required MCPs (render, github) are already active. The fastapi-sso library (/tomasvotava/fastapi-sso, 28 snippets) provides a lightweight Google OAuth adapter for FastAPI that avoids manual authlib/httpx wiring — worth evaluating against raw gotrue-py token exchange. The project already uses supabase-py for the KG store, so the auth integration path is: Supabase Auth (server-side PKCE flow) -> gotrue session token -> itsdangerous signed session cookie -> FastAPI dependency injection for protected routes.
